description_long: This is a weathered stone obelisk with a cylindrical shaft rising from a roughly hewn, rectangular base. The shaft features an inscription carved into its surface, visible on the front and partially on the sides, though the text is eroded and difficult to read. The stone surface is rough and pitted, with visible chips and signs of age, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for a long period. The base is blocky and irregular, contrasting with the more uniform cylindrical shaft above. The tip of the obelisk is rounded and slightly uneven, lacking any pointed or decorative finial. The overall coloration is a pale, sandy beige, typical of limestone or similar sedimentary rock.
description: A weathered cylindrical stone obelisk with a rough rectangular base and eroded inscriptions on its shaft. The surface is pitted and aged, with a rounded, unadorned tip.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main inscription on the shaft and the rectangular, rough base.
description_view_1: Angled side view revealing the curvature of the shaft and partial inscription, with the base's texture visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain, uninscribed back of the shaft and the irregular stone base.
description_view_3: Side view highlighting the worn, rounded tip and the shaft's weathered surface, with faint inscription traces.
attribute_inscription: There is a carved inscription on the front and partially on the sides of the shaft, in a blocky, ancient style. The text is eroded and only partially legible, likely in Latin or Greek.
attribute_base_design: The base is a roughly rectangular block of stone, irregular and unpolished, with a coarse texture and no decorative features.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is rounded and uneven, with no decorative elements or pointed finial.
